ksas.statement: Knowledge of descriptive statistics, including measures of central tendency, dispersion, distribution shape, and correlation, and how to interpret each in a business context.
Knowledge of data visualization best practices, including chart type selection, cognitive load principles, color accessibility, and the difference between exploratory and explanatory visualization.
Knowledge of common analytical frameworks, including cohort analysis, funnel analysis, segmentation, time series analysis, and A/B test result interpretation.
Knowledge of advanced analytical methods including multivariate regression, time series forecasting, clustering, cohort analysis, and experimental design.
Knowledge of A/B testing and experimentation design, including sample size calculation, randomization methods, guardrail metric selection, and statistical significance interpretation.
Knowledge of BI platform capabilities (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, Looker, MicroStrategy) including data model design, calculated field logic, performance optimization, and security implementation.
Knowledge of BI governance practices, including report certification, content lifecycle management, access control, and version control for BI assets.
Knowledge of statistical modeling methods including regression modeling (OLS, GLM, mixed effects), survival analysis, Bayesian modeling, and simulation methods.
Knowledge of causal inference methods, including difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, propensity score matching, and their identification assumptions.
Skill in translating a business question into a data query, identifying the correct data sources, joins, filters, and aggregations required to answer the question accurately.
Skill in producing analysis documentation that is reproducible, clearly reasoned, and accessible to a non-technical business audience.
Skill in designing and executing complex analytical projects independently, including scoping, methodology selection, execution, and executive-level communication of findings.
Skill in mentoring junior analysts, including code and methodology review, feedback delivery, and capability development.
Skill in designing BI semantic models that are performant, reusable, and understandable by business users without requiring technical knowledge.
Skill in building dashboard layouts that communicate KPIs clearly, prioritize decision-relevant information, and scale across device types and user roles.
Skill in designing analytical studies that produce defensible causal or correlational conclusions, with appropriate documentation of assumptions and limitations.

Skill in designing clinical trial databases, including case report form development, edit check logic specification, data validation rules, and the traceability documentation required for regulatory inspection and audit.

Skill in implementing automated data pipeline testing — unit tests for individual transformations, integration tests for end-to-end data flows, and regression tests that validate output consistency across pipeline changes.
Execute analytical requests from business stakeholders, including data extraction, transformation, analysis, and delivery of findings with documented methodology.
Build and maintain self-service reports and dashboards that enable business stakeholders to monitor KPIs and explore data without analyst intervention.
Document analytical methodologies, data sources, business rules, and known data limitations for all recurring analyses and reports.
Lead complex cross-functional analytical projects, coordinating data access, methodology alignment, and stakeholder communication across business units.
Design and analyze controlled experiments (A/B tests), including hypothesis specification, randomization oversight, results interpretation, and recommendation delivery.
Design, build, and publish BI dashboards and reports that meet defined consumer requirements for content, performance, accessibility, and refresh frequency.
Maintain and optimize existing BI assets, including performance tuning, data model updates, and deprecation of unused content.
Manage BI platform governance, including report certification workflows, access control maintenance, and content lifecycle reviews.
Design and execute quantitative analyses for high-stakes business decisions, including methodology selection, sensitivity analysis, and uncertainty quantification.
Produce analytical documentation that meets the evidence standard required for regulatory, audit, or executive decision support contexts.
Monitor data pipeline health dashboards and respond to alerts per defined runbooks, including initial triage, escalation when needed, and incident documentation.
